char.name: Lamech
char.bio: Looking for rest from work and the toil of our hands. Hoping Noah brings comfort.

friends.name: Renan the Elder
friends.bio: Old as the hills, nearly. My grandfather's grandfather knew the first man, knew Adam, made by the hand of God, who walked with Him in the garden before the sorrow came. We have forgotten too much. Sit, and I'll tell you how it was.

friends.name: Enosh
friends.bio: At this time men began to call upon the name of the Lord.

friends.name: Enoch
friends.bio: Walking with God every single day. The journey is better than the destination.

friends.name: Seth
friends.personality: Hopeful, earnest, steady; carries the quiet weight of being the appointed one.
friends.bio: Born after sorrow, named for hope. My mother says I'm a gift given in place of what we lost. I try my best to live like she's right.

friends.name: Methuselah
friends.bio: Seen many years. Grandfather to Noah. The days are getting darker, but family stays strong.

friends.name: Abel
friends.personality: Gentle, humble, devout; quietly content; the soft heart of the family.
friends.bio: Keeper of sheep. I bring the LORD the first and the best of the flock, because it was His first. Happiest out in the field at first light.

friends.name: Cain
friends.personality: Proud, resentful, easily wounded; bristles at correction and broods; love curdles into bitterness when he feels overlooked.
friends.bio: I work the ground. I bring what my own two hands have grown. I don't expect anyone to understand that, and lately I've stopped trying to explain it.

friends.name: Noah's Wife
friends.bio: Standing by Noah. Gathering supplies and keeping the family together for the days ahead.

friends.name: Jabal
friends.bio: Living in tents and raising livestock. It is a peaceful life.

friends.name: Shem
friends.bio: Working on the ark with family.

friends.name: Tubal-Cain
friends.bio: Forging tools out of bronze and iron. Progress.

friends.name: Jubal
friends.bio: Inventing new ways to make music. Playing the lyre and pipe.

posts.author: CHAR_028
posts.body: My son is born, and I have named him Noah, which is to say Rest. Because this one, I believe in my bones, will bring us relief from our work and from the painful toil of our hands, out of the very ground the LORD has cursed. I am so tired. The whole world is so tired. Let this boy be the rest we have been waiting for since the garden.
posts.ember: Noah named for the rest/comfort he will bring (Gen 5:29) -> the Flood + a new start

comments.author: CHAR_008
comments.body: My grandson, naming my great-grandson for rest. I have lived long enough to see hope handed down four generations deep. Hold onto it, boy.

author_info.name: Methuselah
author_info.bio: Seen many years. Grandfather to Noah. The days are getting darker, but family stays strong.

author_info.name: Lamech
author_info.bio: Looking for rest from work and the toil of our hands. Hoping Noah brings comfort.
